Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records, Alternatively, To Enforce Deposition Subpoenas; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,860.00
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jordan Porter for (contestant) Kate Hilburn. Alicia Gamez for (trustee) Charles Odenthal. Paula Canny for Charles Odenthal. Arthur Harris for (defendant) Phyllis Berenson.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Odenthal's capacity as an individual or as trustee, whether or not his deposition would need to be taken twice, that trust funds can only be used if he is named as trustee, and that if Mr. Odenthal is named as trustee on petitions and notices he will comply. Ms. Gamez cited authority regarding the issue (Portico, Hassoldt & Hoiles) and stated that the privacy issue is very important, that as an individual he is a stranger but as a trustee he has the authority to produce the requested documents. Mr. Porter stated that his client is entitled to receive medical records as a matter of law but that they also need the declaration from the Custodian of Record. The Motion for Protective Order Limiting Discovery is denied, the court adopting the tentative ruling. The Motion to Compel Compliance, etc., was taken under submission. Prevailing parties are to provide proposed orders, with Mr. Porter also submitting an amended order for the Motion to Compel Compliance.
